title: Ex-Yankee Sonny Gray earns first victory against New York as member of Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray earned his first win against the Yankees as a Red Sox, leading Boston to a 5-3 victory.
In 6.1 innings, Gray allowed three earned runs, two home runs, and two walks on 79 pitches.
Gray expressed past dislike for the Yankees, saying New York wasn't a good fit for him and his family.

title: Willson Contreras rides highs and lows of Red Sox win over Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a 2-run home run during the Red Sox's win over the Yankees at Yankee Stadium.
Contreras had a confrontation in the dugout, spiking a Gatorade cooler after a collision with Andruw Monasterio.
Contreras leads the Red Sox with 13 home runs and boasts a .299 batting average this season.

title: Severe thunderstorms could hit Mass. Saturday evening, forecasters say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Severe thunderstorms with hail and damaging winds may impact Massachusetts Saturday afternoon and evening, forecasters say.
Greatest risk areas include communities west and northwest of Interstate 95, according to the NWS Boston office.
Severe weather potential extends across southern New England, with possible impacts in Boston between 4 p.m. and 8 p.m.

title: Nantasket Beach stayed quiet despite fears of a social media-fueled ‘takeover’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Hull police increased patrols at Nantasket Beach Friday due to social media rumors but reported no incidents.
Despite concerns, Nantasket Beach had a quieter-than-usual scene with ample parking and beach space available.
Rumors shifted potential gathering location to Boston's Carson Beach, but no incidents were reported there.

title: Red Sox’ Payton Tolle talks ‘playing with joy’ for self, team: ‘I try to come with a smile every day’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle became known after debuting against Pittsburgh, striking out eight in 5.1 innings.
Tolle uses his expressive personality strategically on the field to boost team momentum and play with joy.
Tolle attributes his emotional style to his late mother, advocating to 'play with joy' in baseball.

title: Colin Cowherd: Celtics future feels ‘weird’ compared to sustainable Knicks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Colin Cowherd criticized Celtics' salary cap limitations due to Tatum and Brown's contracts compared to Knicks' flexibility.
Cowherd emphasized the Celtics' dependence on Tatum and Brown, contrasting with Knicks' team-friendly deals and fourth-quarter efficiency.
Cowherd questioned Jaylen Brown's comments on the season, noting team dynamics and contrasting playoff outcomes with the Knicks.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Hoppers' is now streaming on Disney+, featuring a teen using technology to connect with animals.
'Pillion', a unique romcom exploring BDSM dynamics, is streaming on HBO Max.
'Cape Fear', a new series adaptation starring Amy Adams and Patrick Wilson, is streaming on Apple TV.

title: Red Sox move Garrett Crochet to 60-day IL as ace continues to deal with discomfort
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Red Sox moved Garrett Crochet to the 60-day injured list due to ongoing lat discomfort.
The roster move facilitated the addition of reliever Joe La Sorsa to the 40-man roster.
Brayan Bello was demoted to Triple-A Worcester amid Crochet's absence, impacting the starting lineup.

title: Endicott baseball falls to Denison in hard-fought DIII  National Championship Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Endicott College baseball lost to Denison University 4-3 in extra innings in the Division III National Championship Series.
Endicott forced a Game 3 after a dramatic 11-10 walk-off win in Game 2, marking their first national title attempt.
Endicott's baseball appearance in the championship is historic, having won five NCAA Regional Championships and competing in four College World Series.

title: Laxative-laced brownies rattle Nantucket School Committee meeting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Meghan Perry brought laxative-laced brownies to a Nantucket School Committee meeting, highlighting concerns over PFAS in artificial turf.
Perry's gesture was aimed at a proposal to add synthetic grass at Vito Capizzo Stadium, citing health concerns.
No one consumed the brownies; police documented the incident, but potential charges against Perry are unclear.
